info.title: Stopping the cluster services for one node

topic.para: Perform this procedure on the node you want to stop:

step.para: Log in to the node either as the &rootuser; user or as a user with sudo privileges.
Check the status of the cluster:

If the node is in maintenance mode, the resources will keep running even when the cluster services stop, but will stop if you reboot the node. If the node is in standby mode, all the resources have moved to other nodes, so you can safely reboot the node. If the node is in neither mode, the resources will attempt to move to other nodes when you stop the cluster services, but could be caught by a short shutdown timeout, and then you'd have a bad time.
Stop the cluster services on the node:
Check the status of the cluster services:
